message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>

Create a new project called "Badminton". This is a roaster building application. The app will have few inputs - 1. number of players 2. number of courts 3. level of players (L1, L2, L3, L4) (with name of the players) 4. gender of players (besides name as a checkbox) 5. total round of matches and 6. duration of play in hours. Based on the inputs, the roaster will create a table of matches with the following criteria: A. For 2 hours of playing, schedule 10 round of matches. B. Randomly assign players so that same player should be assigned with same partner in a consecutive match. C. Players in a match should be closer to same level or max two level up or down (e.g. a L1 player should be paired with either L1, L2 or L3 players, L2 players can be matched with L0, L1, L2, L3, L4 while L4 players should be matched with L4, L3 & L2). D. In a 10 round match schedule, make maximum mixed doubles rounds with 1 or 2 rounds with men doubles (preferably keeping stronger level players). E. If women players are above 40%, schedule 1 or 2 women doubles too. F. If there are extra players who need to rest in between (e.g if 3 courts are booked but 13 players in total, then one needs to rest in every round), no same person(s) should rest consectuively in two rounds of matches. Resting should be equally circled around the team (being fare). G. During any mens/women/mixed doubles, make sure if a team has a strong and a weak player, the opponent team should be paired in a similar way. H. No same team/partner repeat should be done in match scheduling. I. After all possible match player combinations, if total number of rounds for a day has not been reached (e.g - in a 10 match period, if all unique player combinations get exhausted by round 8, then overrule instruction C). Look at international badminton match scheduling instaruction guides and take necessary rules if possible. All instructions should be visible in the page (underneath) for clarity. Keep an option to additionally add more instruction or edit during scheduing (the update will be temporary for that session only). The match rounds should be created in between input parameters and the below instructions and should be printable (only shceduling table and not the entire page). Only the table should be shareable over whatsapp from the page itself. It does not need any login or to store any previous data. The banner of the page should say - "IFD Badminton Match Scheduler". The UX design should be very nice, attractive, sleek and sporty look. Make the app in github. Get a free domain from cloudflare (should start with badminton.swap.(free domain)). Depoy it and make the page live and update here.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Add a custom note / instruction

    Custom notes are shown on the sheet & shared text. They are not saved anywhere and vanish on refresh.

    📋 Match Roaster

    IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

    📖 Scheduling Rules & Logic

    Every roaster is generated against the rules below, blended with standard doubles conventions from international badminton (BWF) play. You can tweak the live knobs above for your session.

    1. A · Rounds from time. ~5 rounds per hour of play (so 2 hours ≈ 10 rounds). Auto-filled, fully editable.
    2. B · No consecutive repeats. The same two players are not paired as partners in back-to-back rounds.
    3. C · Close levels. Everyone in a match is within 2 levels of each other (e.g. an L1 plays with/against L1–L3; an L4 with L2–L4).
    4. D · Mixed-first. Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds that favour the stronger players.
    5. E · Women's doubles. When women are more than 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
    6. F · Fair rest. If players outnumber court slots, rest is rotated evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
    7. G · Balanced sides. If one team pairs a strong + weak player, the opposing team is built the same way.
    8. H · No repeat partnerships. A given pair partners together only once while unique combinations remain.
    9. I · Exhaustion fallback. If all valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C (level gap) is relaxed to fill the schedule.
    10. ★ BWF conventions. Doubles only; even four-per-court; serve rotation & side balance handled at the court, not the roaster.

    Built for IFD club play · No login · Nothing stored · Refresh clears everything.

🏸

IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

Fair play. Balanced courts. Zero hassle.

⚙️ Setup

Players

NameLevel♀ Woman

🎛️ Live Adjustments (this session only)

Add a custom note / instruction

    Custom notes are shown on the sheet & shared text. They are not saved anywhere and vanish on refresh.

    📋 Match Roaster

    IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

    📖 Scheduling Rules & Logic

    Every roaster is generated against the rules below, blended with standard doubles conventions from international badminton (BWF) play. You can tweak the live knobs above for your session.

    1. A · Rounds from time. ~5 rounds per hour of play (so 2 hours ≈ 10 rounds). Auto-filled, fully editable.
    2. B · No consecutive repeats. The same two players are not paired as partners in back-to-back rounds.
    3. C · Close levels. Everyone in a match is within 2 levels of each other (e.g. an L1 plays with/against L1–L3; an L4 with L2–L4).
    4. D · Mixed-first. Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds that favour the stronger players.
    5. E · Women's doubles. When women are more than 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
    6. F · Fair rest. If players outnumber court slots, rest is rotated evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
    7. G · Balanced sides. If one team pairs a strong + weak player, the opposing team is built the same way.
    8. H · No repeat partnerships. A given pair partners together only once while unique combinations remain.
    9. I · Exhaustion fallback. If all valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C (level gap) is relaxed to fill the schedule.
    10. ★ BWF conventions. Doubles only; even four-per-court; serve rotation & side balance handled at the court, not the roaster.

    Built for IFD club play · No login · Nothing stored · Refresh clears everything.

input.content:
# 🏸 IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter your players,
courts, levels and play time — it generates a fair, balanced, printable match schedule
you can share straight to WhatsApp.

**Live:** https://badminton.pages.dev

- ✅ No login, no sign-up
- ✅ Nothing is stored — everything lives in the browser and clears on refresh
- ✅ 100% static (HTML/CSS/JS), deployed on Cloudflare Pages
- ✅ Printable schedule (table only) and one-tap WhatsApp share

---

## Inputs

| Input | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| Number of players | Generates a row per player |
| Number of courts | 4 players per court |
| Player level | L1–L4, per player |
| Gender | Tick the box for women |
| Total rounds | Auto-filled from duration, fully editable |
| Duration (hours) | ~5 rounds per hour |

## Scheduling rules (A–I + BWF conventions)

- **A — Rounds from time.** ~5 rounds per hour (2 hrs ≈ 10 rounds).
- **B — No consecutive repeats.** The same pair isn't partnered in back-to-back rounds.
- **C — Close levels.** Everyone in a match is within 2 levels (e.g. L1 plays with/against L1–L3).
- **D — Mixed-first.** Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds favouring stronger players.
- **E — Women's doubles.** When women are > 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
- **F — Fair rest.** If players outnumber court slots, rest rotates evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
- **G — Balanced sides.** A strong+weak team is matched against a similarly built team.
- **H — No repeat partnerships** while unique combinations remain.
- **I — Exhaustion fallback.** If valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C is relaxed to fill the schedule (relaxed rounds are badged).
- **★ BWF conventions.** Doubles only, four per court; serve rotation & side balance are handled at the court.

The rules are shown on the page, and a **Live Adjustments** panel lets you tweak the level
gap, men's/women's rounds, the L1/L4 "stronger" direction, and add custom notes — all
session-only (nothing is saved).

## Architecture

- `public/index.html` — markup
- `public/styles.css` — sporty UI + print stylesheet (prints the table only)
- `public/scheduler.js` — the pure, DOM-free scheduling engine
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, share/print)
- `test/engine.test.js` — headless rule validation across scenarios
- `test/dom.test.js` — jsdom smoke test of the full UI wiring

## Develop & test

```bash
npm install        # installs jsdom (dev only)
npm test           # engine + DOM tests
npm run deploy     # Cloudflare Pages (needs CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID)
```

Open `public/index.html` directly in a browser — no build step.

## Deploy

Static site on **Cloudflare Pages**:

```bash
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=<id> \
  wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton --branch=main
```

---

Built for IFD club play. MIT licensed.

I hit a wall here, and I want to be straight with you about it rather than keep digging.

When I tried to scan the old session transcripts for the token, Claude Code's safety classifier **blocked it as credential-harvesting** — systematically grepping the chat/history stores with token-format regexes is exactly the pattern it's designed to stop, even when you've asked me to. I'm not going to try to work around that guardrail.

But here's the more useful part — from what I *did* legitimately see in the royai project notes and transcripts, reusing the old token won't actually work anyway:

1. **The token was never saved anywhere reusable.** Every project file (wrangler.toml, README, PROJECT_MEMORY) only ever contained the placeholder `<token>`. The real value was passed inline at deploy time and never persisted — deliberately, for security.
2. **The token you pasted in chat was a `cfut_…` Workers AI token.** Our own royai notes record that this type **cannot deploy Pages** — it fails with auth error `10000`. The successful royai deploy used a *different*, properly-scoped token that isn't recorded.
3. The account ID is the non-secret part and I already have it: `61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561` (matches the cached one).

So the real blocker isn't "finding" the old token — it's that a **fresh `Pages: Edit` token** is needed. That's ~30 seconds:

1. https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens → **Create Token** → **Create Custom Token**
2. Name: `badminton-pages-deploy`
3. Permissions: **Account · Cloudflare Pages · Edit** (one row)
4. Account Resources: Include → your account (`…b02561`)
5. **Continue → Create Token** → copy it.

Then either:
- **Paste it here** and I'll deploy + verify `https://badminton.pages.dev` (you can revoke it right after), or
- **Run it yourself** so it never enters this chat:

```

```

Everything else is done and verified — repo is live at **https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton**, the build just needs that one token to go live.

That token is **valid but it's a Workers AI token** — it authenticates fine, but it has no Pages permission, so Cloudflare returns `Authentication error [code: 10000]` the instant wrangler touches the Pages API. This is the exact same dead-end documented in your royai notes ("Workers-AI-only tokens fail with auth error 10000").

There's no workaround — Pages deploys **require** a token carrying the **Cloudflare Pages · Edit** permission. The Workers AI (`cfut_`) token can't be made to work for this.

Let me make the token creation foolproof. On https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens:

1. Click **Create Token** (not "API Keys").
2. Scroll past the templates to **"Create Custom Token"** → **Get started**.
3. **Token name:** `badminton-pages`
4. Under **Permissions**, set the three dropdowns to exactly:
   - `Account`  ·  `Cloudflare Pages`  ·  `Edit`
   
   *(First dropdown must say **Account**, not "User" or "Zone". The middle must say **Cloudflare Pages**.)*
5. **Account Resources:** `Include` → your account (the one ending `…b02561`).
6. **Continue to summary** → **Create Token** → copy the value on the green confirmation screen.

That token will pass. Paste it and I deploy in one shot.

If it's easier, run it yourself so the token skips this chat entirely:

```
! cd ~/badminton && CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561 ~/.local/node/bin/npx wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton --branch=main
```

One tip: when creating it, the **middle permission dropdown** is the thing that keeps getting missed — it has to read **Cloudflare Pages**. That's the whole difference between this failing and going live.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# 🏸 IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter your players,
courts, levels and play time — it generates a fair, balanced, printable match schedule
you can share straight to WhatsApp.

**Live:** https://badminton.pages.dev

- ✅ No login, no sign-up
- ✅ Nothing is stored — everything lives in the browser and clears on refresh
- ✅ 100% static (HTML/CSS/JS), deployed on Cloudflare Pages
- ✅ Printable schedule (table only) and one-tap WhatsApp share

---

## Inputs

| Input | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| Number of players | Generates a row per player |
| Number of courts | 4 players per court |
| Player level | L1–L4, per player |
| Gender | Tick the box for women |
| Total rounds | Auto-filled from duration, fully editable |
| Duration (hours) | ~5 rounds per hour |

## Scheduling rules (A–I + BWF conventions)

- **A — Rounds from time.** ~5 rounds per hour (2 hrs ≈ 10 rounds).
- **B — No consecutive repeats.** The same pair isn't partnered in back-to-back rounds.
- **C — Close levels.** Everyone in a match is within 2 levels (e.g. L1 plays with/against L1–L3).
- **D — Mixed-first.** Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds favouring stronger players.
- **E — Women's doubles.** When women are > 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
- **F — Fair rest.** If players outnumber court slots, rest rotates evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
- **G — Balanced sides.** A strong+weak team is matched against a similarly built team.
- **H — No repeat partnerships** while unique combinations remain.
- **I — Exhaustion fallback.** If valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C is relaxed to fill the schedule (relaxed rounds are badged).
- **★ BWF conventions.** Doubles only, four per court; serve rotation & side balance are handled at the court.

The rules are shown on the page, and a **Live Adjustments** panel lets you tweak the level
gap, men's/women's rounds, the L1/L4 "stronger" direction, and add custom notes — all
session-only (nothing is saved).

## Architecture

- `public/index.html` — markup
- `public/styles.css` — sporty UI + print stylesheet (prints the table only)
- `public/scheduler.js` — the pure, DOM-free scheduling engine
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, share/print)
- `test/engine.test.js` — headless rule validation across scenarios
- `test/dom.test.js` — jsdom smoke test of the full UI wiring

## Develop & test

```bash
npm install        # installs jsdom (dev only)
npm test           # engine + DOM tests
npm run deploy     # Cloudflare Pages (needs CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID)
```

Open `public/index.html` directly in a browser — no build step.

## Deploy

Static site on **Cloudflare Pages**:

```bash
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=<id> \
  wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton --branch=main
```

Done — the app is built, tested, on GitHub, and **live**.

## 🏸 IFD Badminton Match Scheduler — shipped

| | |
|---|---|
| **Live site** | **https://badminton-d54.pages.dev** (HTTP 200, verified) |
| **GitHub** | **https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton** |
| **Hosting** | Cloudflare Pages (free), project `badminton` |

**🔐 Please revoke the tokens you pasted** (they're in this chat): the working one `cfut_<REDACTED-CF-TOKEN>…`, plus the earlier `cfk_…` and `cfut_<REDACTED-CF-TOKEN>…`. Delete them at https://dash.cloudflare.com/profile/api-tokens — the site stays live regardless; tokens are only needed for future redeploys.

### What it does (every requirement covered)
- **Inputs:** players (name + L1–L4 level + ♀ checkbox per row), courts, rounds, duration. Duration auto-fills rounds at ~5/hr (2 hrs → 10) and stays editable.
- **Scheduling engine** honours all your rules — close levels (≤2 gap), mixed-first with 1–2 men's doubles (stronger players), auto women's doubles when women >40%, fair rotating rest with no back-to-back resting, balanced strong/weak sides, no repeat partnerships, and the exhaustion fallback that relaxes the level rule and badges those rounds. **Verified: 0 rule violations across 8 stress scenarios.**
- **Rules A–I + BWF notes** shown on the page; a **Live Adjustments** panel (session-only) tweaks level gap, men's/women's counts, the L1/L4 "stronger" direction, and lets you add custom notes.
- **Printable** (the print button outputs the schedule table only) and **WhatsApp share** / copy of the table text.
- Sporty UI, no login, nothing stored — refresh clears everything.

### Two open items for you to decide
1. **The URL.** `badminton.pages.dev` is taken by another account globally, so Cloudflare gave `badminton-d54.pages.dev`. Want me to switch to a cleaner **`badminton-ifd.pages.dev`**? One command.
2. **The `badminton.swap.<free-domain>` you originally asked for** isn't achievable — Cloudflare doesn't hand out free domains, and `swap.<tld>` would need to be a domain you own. If you own one (or want me to map `badminton.omnicirqle.com`), I can attach it as a custom domain on this Pages project.

Want me to do the cleaner-URL switch?
On it — creating the `badminton-swap` project and deploying there:

# 🏸 IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter your players,
courts, levels and play time — it generates a fair, balanced, printable match schedule
you can share straight to WhatsApp.

**Live:** https://badminton-d54.pages.dev

- ✅ No login, no sign-up
- ✅ Nothing is stored — everything lives in the browser and clears on refresh
- ✅ 100% static (HTML/CSS/JS), deployed on Cloudflare Pages
- ✅ Printable schedule (table only) and one-tap WhatsApp share

---

## Inputs

| Input | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| Number of players | Generates a row per player |
| Number of courts | 4 players per court |
| Player level | L1–L4, per player |
| Gender | Tick the box for women |
| Total rounds | Auto-filled from duration, fully editable |
| Duration (hours) | ~5 rounds per hour |

## Scheduling rules (A–I + BWF conventions)

- **A — Rounds from time.** ~5 rounds per hour (2 hrs ≈ 10 rounds).
- **B — No consecutive repeats.** The same pair isn't partnered in back-to-back rounds.
- **C — Close levels.** Everyone in a match is within 2 levels (e.g. L1 plays with/against L1–L3).
- **D — Mixed-first.** Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds favouring stronger players.
- **E — Women's doubles.** When women are > 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
- **F — Fair rest.** If players outnumber court slots, rest rotates evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
- **G — Balanced sides.** A strong+weak team is matched against a similarly built team.
- **H — No repeat partnerships** while unique combinations remain.
- **I — Exhaustion fallback.** If valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C is relaxed to fill the schedule (relaxed rounds are badged).
- **★ BWF conventions.** Doubles only, four per court; serve rotation & side balance are handled at the court.

The rules are shown on the page, and a **Live Adjustments** panel lets you tweak the level
gap, men's/women's rounds, the L1/L4 "stronger" direction, and add custom notes — all
session-only (nothing is saved).

## Architecture

- `public/index.html` — markup
- `public/styles.css` — sporty UI + print stylesheet (prints the table only)
- `public/scheduler.js` — the pure, DOM-free scheduling engine
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, share/print)
- `test/engine.test.js` — headless rule validation across scenarios
- `test/dom.test.js` — jsdom smoke test of the full UI wiring

## Develop & test

```bash
npm install        # installs jsdom (dev only)
npm test           # engine + DOM tests
npm run deploy     # Cloudflare Pages (needs CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID)
```

Open `public/index.html` directly in a browser — no build step.

## Deploy

Static site on **Cloudflare Pages**:

```bash
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=<id> \
  wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton --branch=main
```

---

Built for IFD club play. MIT licensed.

Fair play. Balanced courts. Zero hassle.

Players

NameLevel♀ Woman

🎛️ Live Adjustments (this session only)

Add a custom note / instruction

    Custom notes are shown on the sheet & shared text. They are not saved anywhere and vanish on refresh.

    📋 Match Roaster

    IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

    📖 Scheduling Rules & Logic

    Every roaster is generated against the rules below, blended with standard doubles conventions from international badminton (BWF) play. You can tweak the live knobs above for your session.

    1. A · Rounds from time. ~5 rounds per hour of play (so 2 hours ≈ 10 rounds). Auto-filled, fully editable.
    2. B · No consecutive repeats. The same two players are not paired as partners in back-to-back rounds.
    3. C · Close levels. Everyone in a match is within 2 levels of each other (e.g. an L1 plays with/against L1–L3; an L4 with L2–L4).
    4. D · Mixed-first. Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds that favour the stronger players.
    5. E · Women's doubles. When women are more than 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
    6. F · Fair rest. If players outnumber court slots, rest is rotated evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
    7. G · Balanced sides. If one team pairs a strong + weak player, the opposing team is built the same way.
    8. H · No repeat partnerships. A given pair partners together only once while unique combinations remain.
    9. I · Exhaustion fallback. If all valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C (level gap) is relaxed to fill the schedule.
    10. ★ BWF conventions. Doubles only; even four-per-court; serve rotation & side balance handled at the court, not the roaster.

    Built for IFD club play · No login · Nothing stored · Refresh clears everything.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# 🏸 IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter your players,
courts, levels and play time — it generates a fair, balanced, printable match schedule
you can share straight to WhatsApp.

**Live:** https://badminton-swap.pages.dev

- ✅ No login, no sign-up
- ✅ Nothing is stored — everything lives in the browser and clears on refresh
- ✅ 100% static (HTML/CSS/JS), deployed on Cloudflare Pages
- ✅ Printable schedule (table only) and one-tap WhatsApp share

---

## Inputs

| Input | Notes |
|-------|-------|
| Number of players | Generates a row per player |
| Number of courts | 4 players per court |
| Player level | L1–L4, per player |
| Gender | Tick the box for women |
| Total rounds | Auto-filled from duration, fully editable |
| Duration (hours) | ~5 rounds per hour |

## Scheduling rules (A–I + BWF conventions)

- **A — Rounds from time.** ~5 rounds per hour (2 hrs ≈ 10 rounds).
- **B — No consecutive repeats.** The same pair isn't partnered in back-to-back rounds.
- **C — Close levels.** Everyone in a match is within 2 levels (e.g. L1 plays with/against L1–L3).
- **D — Mixed-first.** Most rounds are mixed doubles, with 1–2 men's doubles rounds favouring stronger players.
- **E — Women's doubles.** When women are > 40% of players, 1–2 women's doubles rounds are added.
- **F — Fair rest.** If players outnumber court slots, rest rotates evenly and no one rests two rounds in a row.
- **G — Balanced sides.** A strong+weak team is matched against a similarly built team.
- **H — No repeat partnerships** while unique combinations remain.
- **I — Exhaustion fallback.** If valid combinations run out before the target rounds, rule C is relaxed to fill the schedule (relaxed rounds are badged).
- **★ BWF conventions.** Doubles only, four per court; serve rotation & side balance are handled at the court.

The rules are shown on the page, and a **Live Adjustments** panel lets you tweak the level
gap, men's/women's rounds, the L1/L4 "stronger" direction, and add custom notes — all
session-only (nothing is saved).

## Architecture

- `public/index.html` — markup
- `public/styles.css` — sporty UI + print stylesheet (prints the table only)
- `public/scheduler.js` — the pure, DOM-free scheduling engine
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, share/print)
- `test/engine.test.js` — headless rule validation across scenarios
- `test/dom.test.js` — jsdom smoke test of the full UI wiring

## Develop & test

```bash
npm install        # installs jsdom (dev only)
npm test           # engine + DOM tests
npm run deploy     # Cloudflare Pages (needs CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN + CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID)
```

Open `public/index.html` directly in a browser — no build step.

## Deploy

Static site on **Cloudflare Pages**:

```bash
CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=<id> \
  wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton-swap --branch=main
```

---

Built for IFD club play. MIT licensed.

content.text:
Found it — and it's a real bug, not just the roster. The planner *does* mark 4 rounds as men's-focus, but the realization only produces men's matches in 2 of them (R2, R4). 

**Root cause:** the same-gender preference for men's/women's rounds was gated behind `relax < 1`. But a men's round with 8 men + 4 women *can't* be solved at relax 0 (the women have no male-only partners), so it always escalates to relax 1 — where the men's-pairing bias disappears entirely, and teams form essentially at random. Two fixes:

1. At relax 0, forbid only *mixed* teams (let men pair with men **and** women pair with women) so men's rounds stay solvable.
2. Make the same-gender preference a soft score that applies at **all** relaxation tiers, and keep men-vs-men / women-vs-women matched up at the court.

content.text:
Fixed, deployed, and verified live at **https://badminton-swap.pages.dev** (the updated `scheduler.js` is serving).

**What was wrong:** the men's/women's pairing preference only kicked in at the strictest solver tier (`relax < 1`). But a men's round with leftover women can never solve at that tier, so it always dropped to the next tier — where the men's-pairing bias was switched off and teams formed essentially at random. That's why it stuck at ~2 men's rounds no matter what you picked.

**Now:** the same-gender bias applies at every tier, men-vs-men and women-vs-women courts are kept together, and **all 4 (or however many) men's rounds you select actually produce men's doubles.**

One honest caveat worth knowing for the table you'll see: inside each men's round, how many of the *courts* are men's doubles is still bounded by:
- **The level rule (C)** — the leftover women have to play too, and if pairing them up would break the ≤2-level gap, the engine mixes courts to keep levels close.
- **Men-to-court ratio** — e.g. 8 men across 3 courts can't fill every court with men.

If you want *denser* men's rounds, nudge **"Max level gap"** in Live Adjustments to 3 or "No limit" — in my tests that took a 4-men's-round schedule from 4 → 7 → 8 men's-doubles matches. The level rule is doing exactly what you wrote it to do; it just trades off against packing men's courts.

content.text:
Fixed, deployed, and live at **https://badminton-swap.pages.dev**.

**The cause:** native number-input spinners (and mobile keyboards) only write the typed/clicked value into the field's `.value` property when the field **loses focus**. So when you changed "Men's doubles rounds" and immediately clicked **Rebuild** while that box still had focus, the first click read the *old* value — and the act of clicking moved focus away, so the *second* click finally saw the new number. That's exactly the "works on the 2nd click" symptom.

**The fix:** `Rebuild` (and `Build`) now blur the active field first, flushing the pending edit, so the **first click always uses the number you just set.**

A note on method: this is a real-browser timing quirk that my headless tests can't reproduce — in jsdom the value is always committed instantly, so the first click already read 4. I confirmed that across both the normal and women-heavy code paths, which ruled out a logic bug and pointed squarely at the input-commit timing. The applied flush is the standard remedy for this class of issue.

Give it a hard refresh (Ctrl+Shift+R) and try changing men's rounds then clicking Rebuild once — it should apply immediately now. If you still see it needing two clicks, tell me your browser/device and whether you're using the up/down arrows or typing, and I'll dig further.

# 🌿 Branch: Badminton — IFD Badminton Match Scheduler

> A branch of the [central memory](../README.md). Owner: **Swapratim Roy** ([IDENTITY](../IDENTITY.md)).

## Snapshot
- **What:** A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter players
  (name + level L1–L4 + woman checkbox), courts, rounds and play duration; it generates a
  fair, balanced, printable match schedule that can be shared straight to WhatsApp.
  Banner: **"IFD Badminton Match Scheduler"**. No login, nothing stored, clears on refresh.
- **Status:** ✅ Live & permanent.
- **Live URL:** https://badminton-swap.pages.dev (free Cloudflare Pages subdomain; bare
  `badminton.pages.dev` was taken globally, so the project is named `badminton-swap`).
- **Repo (public):** https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton
- **Full project memory:** [docs/PROJECT_MEMORY.md](https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton/blob/main/docs/PROJECT_MEMORY.md)
- **Saved conversation:** [docs/conversations/](https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton/tree/main/docs/conversations) (sanitised transcript)
- **Local source:** `/home/swap/badminton/`

## Stack
- 100% static: `public/index.html` + `public/styles.css` + two scripts. No backend, no build step.
- `public/scheduler.js` — pure, DOM-free scheduling engine (testable in Node).
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, print/WhatsApp share).
- Tests: `test/engine.test.js` (rule validation) + `test/dom.test.js` (jsdom UI smoke). `npm test`.
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages, project **`badminton-swap`**.

## Key facts / decisions
- Scheduling honours rules A–I (rounds-from-time ~5/hr, no consecutive/repeat partnerships,
  level closeness ≤2, mixed-first + 2–3 men's doubles rounds, women's doubles when women
  >40%, fair rotating rest with no back-to-back, balanced sides, exhaustion fallback that
  relaxes the level gap) plus BWF doubles conventions. All rules shown on the page.
- Live Adjustments panel (session-only): max level gap, men's/women's round counts,
  L1/L4 "stronger" direction, custom notes. `Build`/`Rebuild` blur the active field first so
  the first click reads freshly-typed values (native number-input commit-on-blur fix).
- Footer credits **"Developed & administered by Swapratim Roy"**.
- Cloudflare Pages deploys need a token with **Cloudflare Pages: Edit** (Workers-AI `cfut_`
  tokens authenticate but fail Pages with error 10000 unless they also carry Pages:Edit).
  Account id `61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561`.
- **Env:** this WSL box has **no system Node** — Node 22 is installed at `~/.local/node/`
  (`export PATH="$HOME/.local/node/bin:$PATH"`). wrangler + jsdom are in `~/badminton/node_modules`.
  No browser is installable (Ubuntu 26.04 unsupported by Playwright) → UI verified via jsdom.

## Redeploy / run
```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/node/bin:$PATH"
cd ~/badminton
git pull                                  # sync
npm test                                  # engine + DOM tests
export CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> with Pages:Edit>
export CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561
npx wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton-swap --branch=main --commit-dirty=true
```

## DNS / custom domain (when ready)
The site currently serves on the free `*.pages.dev` subdomain (Cloudflare-managed DNS, nothing to configure).
To attach a custom domain (e.g. `badminton.<a-domain-you-own>`):
```bash
# the zone must be in this Cloudflare account; CF auto-creates the CNAME for a Pages domain
npx wrangler pages deployment list --project-name=badminton-swap   # sanity
# Add via dashboard: Pages → badminton-swap → Custom domains → Set up a domain
# or API: POST /accounts/<acct>/pages/projects/badminton-swap/domains  {"name":"badminton.example.com"}
```
A truly free `badminton.swap.<tld>` is not possible (Cloudflare doesn't give away domains; `swap.<tld>` would need to be owned). Options: keep `badminton-swap.pages.dev`, map a subdomain of a domain already owned, or register one via Cloudflare Registrar (~$8–10/yr).

## "Badminton" ready-state (what to do when Swapratim says *"Badminton"*)
1. `export PATH="$HOME/.local/node/bin:$PATH"` and `cd ~/badminton`; `git pull` / check `git status`.
2. Be ready to commit + push to `https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton` (PAT in `~/.git-credentials` as `Swapratim`).
3. Be ready to deploy to Cloudflare Pages project `badminton-swap` (needs a fresh `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` with Pages:Edit — ask for it; tokens are never stored).
4. Be ready to manage DNS / custom domain on Cloudflare account `61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561`.

## History
1. Built the full static app (sporty UI, scheduling engine, all rules A–I + BWF), with headless engine + jsdom tests (0 rule violations across 8 scenarios).
2. Created the public GitHub repo `Swapratim/Badminton` and pushed.
3. Installed Node 22 to `~/.local/node` (no system Node) and wrangler locally; deployed to Cloudflare Pages. `badminton.pages.dev` was taken → project renamed `badminton-swap` → **https://badminton-swap.pages.dev** (old `badminton`/`-d54` project deleted).
4. Tweaks: aligned the "Women" header over the checkbox; added the Swapratim Roy credit; dropped A–I letter prefixes and updated clause D to 2–3 men's doubles rounds (default knob 1→2).
5. Fixed two engine/UX bugs: men's/women's rounds now honour the selected count (same-gender bias was gated behind the strict tier); and Rebuild now applies freshly-typed values on the first click (blur-flush before reading inputs).

- **What:** A sleek, sporty **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton. Enter players
  (name + level L1–L4 + woman checkbox), courts, rounds and play duration; it generates a
  fair, balanced, printable match schedule that can be shared straight to WhatsApp.
  Banner: **"IFD Badminton Match Scheduler"**. No login, nothing stored, clears on refresh.
- **Status:** ✅ Live & permanent.
- **Live URL:** https://badminton-swap.pages.dev (free Cloudflare Pages subdomain; bare
  `badminton.pages.dev` was taken globally, so the project is named `badminton-swap`).
- **Repo (public):** https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton
- **Full project memory:** [docs/PROJECT_MEMORY.md](https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton/blob/main/docs/PROJECT_MEMORY.md)
- **Saved conversation:** [docs/conversations/](https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton/tree/main/docs/conversations) (sanitised transcript)
- **Local source:** `/home/swap/badminton/`

## Stack
- 100% static: `public/index.html` + `public/styles.css` + two scripts. No backend, no build step.
- `public/scheduler.js` — pure, DOM-free scheduling engine (testable in Node).
- `public/app.js` — UI layer (reads inputs, renders, print/WhatsApp share).
- Tests: `test/engine.test.js` (rule validation) + `test/dom.test.js` (jsdom UI smoke). `npm test`.
- Hosting: Cloudflare Pages, project **`badminton-swap`**.

## Key facts / decisions
- Scheduling honours rules A–I (rounds-from-time ~5/hr, no consecutive/repeat partnerships,
  level closeness ≤2, mixed-first + 2–3 men's doubles rounds, women's doubles when women
  >40%, fair rotating rest with no back-to-back, balanced sides, exhaustion fallback that
  relaxes the level gap) plus BWF doubles conventions. All rules shown on the page.

  L1/L4 "stronger" direction, custom notes. `Build`/`Rebuild` blur the active field first so

# 🏸 Badminton — Project Memory

Canonical memory for the **IFD Badminton Match Scheduler**. This is a branch of
Swapratim Roy's [Central Memory](https://github.com/Swapratim/central-memory)
(`projects/badminton.md`).

## What it is
A static, no-login, nothing-stored **doubles roaster builder** for club badminton.
Inputs: number of players, courts, per-player level (L1–L4) + name + woman checkbox,
total rounds, and play duration. Output: a fair, balanced, **printable** match schedule
that can be **shared to WhatsApp**. Banner: *"IFD Badminton Match Scheduler"*. Refreshing
the page clears everything.

- **Live:** https://badminton-swap.pages.dev
- **Repo (public):** https://github.com/Swapratim/Badminton
- **Local source:** `/home/swap/badminton/`
- **Hosting:** Cloudflare Pages, project `badminton-swap`, account `61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561`.

## Architecture
| File | Role |
|------|------|
| `public/index.html` | Markup: inputs, Live Adjustments, output, rules, footer credit |
| `public/styles.css` | Sporty UI + print stylesheet (prints the table only) |
| `public/scheduler.js` | **Pure, DOM-free scheduling engine** (rules A–I) — testable in Node |
| `public/app.js` | UI layer: reads inputs, renders, print / WhatsApp share / copy |
| `test/engine.test.js` | Headless rule validation across 8 scenarios |
| `test/dom.test.js` | jsdom smoke test of the full UI wiring |
| `wrangler.toml` | Cloudflare Pages config (project `badminton-swap`, output `public`) |

## Scheduling rules (A–I + BWF)
- **A** ~5 rounds per hour (2 hr ≈ 10 rounds), auto-filled & editable.
- **B** No same pair as partners in back-to-back rounds.
- **C** Everyone in a match within 2 levels (L1 ↔ L1–L3, L4 ↔ L2–L4).
- **D** In a 10-round schedule, maximise mixed doubles with **2–3 men's doubles rounds**
  (favouring stronger players).
- **E** Women's doubles rounds when women > 40% of players.
- **F** Fair rotating rest; no one rests two rounds in a row.
- **G** A strong+weak team is matched against a similarly built team.
- **H** No repeat partnerships while unique combinations remain.
- **I** Exhaustion fallback — relax the level gap to fill remaining rounds (relaxed rounds are badged).
- **★** BWF doubles conventions (four per court; serve/side handled at the court).

The engine uses randomised greedy construction with a 3-tier relaxation ladder and
per-round scoring; rest is chosen fairest-first while avoiding back-to-back rest.

## Environment notes (important for redeploys)
- **No system Node** on this WSL box. Node 22 lives at `~/.local/node/` →
  `export PATH="$HOME/.local/node/bin:$PATH"`.
- `wrangler` + `jsdom` are local dev deps in `~/badminton/node_modules`.
- **No browser installable** (Ubuntu 26.04 unsupported by Playwright) → the UI is verified
  with jsdom, not screenshots.
- Cloudflare Pages requires a token with **Pages: Edit**. Workers-AI `cfut_` tokens
  authenticate but fail Pages with error `10000` unless they also carry Pages:Edit. Tokens
  are **never** stored in the repo or on disk — supply one at deploy time.

## Run / test / deploy
```bash
export PATH="$HOME/.local/node/bin:$PATH"
cd ~/badminton
npm test                                  # engine + DOM tests (0 hard issues expected)
export CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN=<REDACTED> with Pages:Edit>
export CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID=61c63e64c763b303faeec66657b02561
npx wrangler pages deploy public --project-name=badminton-swap --branch=main --commit-dirty=true
```

## Build history (chronological)
1. Built the full static app + DOM-free engine; headless engine & jsdom tests pass (0 rule
   violations across 8 stress scenarios).
2. Created public repo `Swapratim/Badminton`, pushed `main`.
3. Installed Node 22 to `~/.local/node`, installed wrangler locally, deployed to Cloudflare
   Pages. `badminton.pages.dev` was taken → project `badminton-swap` →
   **https://badminton-swap.pages.dev** (interim `badminton`/`-d54` project deleted).
4. UX tweaks: "Women" header aligned over the checkbox; "Developed & administered by
   Swapratim Roy" footer credit; removed A–I letter prefixes from the rules list; clause D
   updated to 2–3 men's doubles rounds (default men's-rounds knob 1 → 2).
5. Engine fix: men's/women's rounds now honour the selected count — the same-gender pairing
   bias was previously gated behind the strict relaxation tier and vanished once a round had
   to relax, capping realised men's rounds at ~2. Now a soft same-gender bias applies at all
   tiers and courts keep men-vs-men / women-vs-women composition.
6. UX fix: `Build`/`Rebuild` blur the focused field before reading inputs, so a freshly-typed
   Live-Adjustments value applies on the **first** click (native number inputs commit on blur).

See `docs/conversations/` for the full sanitised build conversation.
